item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean… If they were just people who got killed, why would the organization go out of their way to preserve their bodies via formaldehyde instead of just getting rid of the evidence?

item.title: Admin Post - Disciplinary - Open
item.content:encoded:
friskynisky, if you believe an article should use a certain formatting element, contact the author directly via wikidot PM.

Give how many pages you can asked the same question on, this is an official warning. Please do not spam comments asking to add alternate formatting to pages.

item.title: Acs?
item.content:encoded: Can I add an ACS heading?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm confused, I thought the bodies in Document 3325-1 were just the mad science organization's employees who'd either been mauled by mutated mascot monsters or outlived their usefulness/knew too much and been shot. Are you suggesting the mad science organization was using them as raw materials for making the mascots?
oh yea!! happy to see this in the 100+'s! love it love it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was creepy from the start and got even creepier when the last addendum came and I realized why they needed those bodies. This is a pretty balanced skip; it doesn't tell you everything but gives enough information to cause some very disturbing thoughts.

Very well done. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aspiring writers, take notes. This is how you do horror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, reverse FNAF.

In FNAF they put humans into animatronics. In this one thet bred people into animatronics.

Personally, as an American, I've seen the FNAF plot one too many times so the vengeful spirit troor is completely worn out. I like this play on it more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Um personally I don't think so. My own personal theory is that the researchers took the corpses, which all seem to be adult, researched the cells and their makeup (or stole other research done) and synthesized new life forms. They directly evolved these life forms to serve the purpose of a real life TV show (or animatronic, I could go on for hours about that). The research seems to have been shut down before children actors were ever used. Of course the original author can confirm or deny this, but those are my thoughts on the matter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't mean literally… what I mean is that we have kids party place with malicious artificial life forms that look like mascots

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, it's not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is… is it FNAF?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loving this one for the most part. Some of the puppet descriptions are a little on the nose for me though. I feel like these being "off-brand" characters over famous puppets/muppets would be a nice touch.

As I was reading the logs I definitely got a sense of dread of what was to come at the end, what the big reveal would be on the lower floor. Had figured it was going to be something about the company moving on from just puppet monsters to puppet people or something. From what I get of it, they were losing/terminating more personnel than previously thought?

It already seemed like they were having a lot of breaches and attacks, so I'm not sure how much greater the death count would be there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the link!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much, it was a fun idea to explore and write.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is exactly how I found this article. Glad I watched the video.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I imagine this article is about to get it’s fair share of attention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one a lot! Very well done, and creepy, good job Author.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Part of me is thinking that the children's show story was a blind for the real purpose of creating living cthonic abominations.
On second thought, having this happening as a result of an attempt to create actual living cartoon/puppet characters might be even more disturbing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been thinking of working on pictures for the SCP-3325-1 instances, but I can't really find the time to touch them up nicely, and end up rushing them.

I'll add a link to a picture SCP-3325-1a, in its underdeveloped form. It was made in Mudbox 2017, but I added the distortions later, to try to 'cheat' and hide the imperfections. Ultimately, it wasn't developed enough to be included in the article, which is sad.

http://scpsandbox2.wdfiles.com/local--files/redemptioninitiative/freaky_beaky.jpg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, glad you enjoyed the read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the way the men in black act like a mirror Foundation - similarly cold and scientific, but even less constrained by ethics than the foundation, and purposefully creating anomalies rather than containing them. The causes of death in the final log drove the connection home for me.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh dear, I just realized this article could have been much creepier, after discussing with my associate.

He suggested a part where one of the staff locked himself in the closet with the "characters" scratching at the door and chanting phrases while he breaks down mentally.

That would have been a nice touch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For the sake of life on Earth, those two items should never come into contact with one another.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cross-test proposal: SCP-1435 x SCP-3325
Status: denied by O5 council on grounds of "No. Just…no." 11/2 vote1
Notes: Dissenting opinions included denial on grounds of "oh hell no" and "potential depletion of class-F amnestic reserves"

Footnotes
1. While a tiebreaking vote was unnecessary, O5-13 nonetheless manifested and entered an vote in favor of denial

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was thinking of making a picture of the facility, but not the characters.

Edit: Here's a picture I made of SCP-3325-1a in embryonic form. Ultimately, I left it out because I didn't work on it enough for it too look realistic: http://scpsandbox2.wdfiles.com/local--files/redemptioninitiative/freaky_beaky.jpg

It's inspired by Big Bird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a question, are you thinking of making a picture for this like your other works?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always like some FNAF like horror, mascot costumes creep me out because I don't know what's inside of them, plus it's kinda weird to see something act like a cartoon character, but not speak.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Five nights at Freddy's mixed with Dr Moreau. Love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you very much, I'll see if I can make some minor adjustments that may improve the article.

By the way, I loved your puppet skip, it was really weird/awesome!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the feedback.

True, the security camera feeds may be a tad comedic, but I've seen people mess around in that tone, so I thought I'd risk it and add it in.

I'll see if I can make some minor adjustments.

Edit: fixed some of the errors, thank you very much for pointing them out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Addendum 2 is pretty weak, and I'm not sure I get anything out of the list of causes of death in the last one. Also, watch in part 3 for logs that go on too long; if there's a security breach and the guy's supposed to be doing something, I wouldn't expect he'd get much more than "Shit!" out before killing the recording.

That said, this is nicely creepy and has a good backstory, and I'm upvoting out of narcissism if nothing else, being (I believe) the only other person who's written a Muppet-based skip. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great. My only issue is that the log of the guards messing around and then directly telling the person behind the security camera feeds to erase the log is just a little unbelievable and tone-breakingly comedic, especially with the camera literally nodding.

There's also a less/fewer error in the containment procedures, and:

Researcher: God damn it! None of the higher ups aren't taking me seriously!

aren't/are mix-up.

Anyway, yeah. Minor qualms aside, this is super disturbing, well-done horror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for your review, glad you enjoyed the read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very glad to see this up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Weird article that basically involves living children's show characters gone wrong.

Hope you like it.
